Other significant single-company pharmaceutical settlements include:

*   **GlaxoSmithKline (GSK)**: $3 billion in 2012 for unlawfully promoting prescription drugs and failing to report safety information[1](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [2](https://www.enjuris.com/blog/resources/largest-pharmaceutical-settlements-lawsuits/).
*   **Pfizer**: $2.3 billion in 2009 for the illegal promotion of certain pharmaceutical products, including the off-label promotion of Bextra[1](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [3](https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-announces-largest-health-care-fraud-settlement-its-history).
*   **Johnson & Johnson**: $2.2 billion in 2013 for misbranding the antipsychotic drug Risperdal, including allegations of off-label marketing and kickbacks[1](https://www.oal-law.com/blog/5-largest-pharmaceutical-lawsuits/), [4](https://www.cpmlegal.com/blogs-Advocates-For-Justice/what-are-the-top-pharmaceutical-litigation-settlements).
